name: Miami
summary: Miami is a sunny Florida city beside the warm Atlantic Ocean, famous for white-sand beaches and pastel Art Deco hotels. Just an hour away, wild alligators sunbathe in the giant Everglades wetlands.
funFacts: South Beach has the biggest collection of Art Deco buildings in the world, with more than 800 colourful pastel hotels built in the 1920s and 1930s.
PortMiami is nicknamed the 'Cruise Capital of the World' and welcomes more cruise ship passengers than any other port on Earth.
In Miami's Little Havana neighbourhood you'll hear Spanish more than English, because lots of families came from the island of Cuba.
Wild American alligators live in the Everglades just west of Miami, where airboats whoosh across the sawgrass swamps to spot them.
Miami is so warm that mangoes, coconuts and papayas grow in people's gardens, and bright pink flamingos really do live in Florida.
thingsToDo: Stroll along Ocean Drive in South Beach to spot the curvy pink, mint and lemon-yellow Art Deco hotels.
Build sandcastles at Miami Beach and wave to the lifeguards in their bright pastel-coloured wooden towers.
Tour Vizcaya Museum and Gardens, an old Italian-style mansion on Biscayne Bay with fountains and a stone barge in the sea.
Wander Wynwood Walls to see giant rainbow-coloured murals painted by street artists from all around the world.
Eat a guava-and-cheese pastelito on Calle Ocho in Little Havana while listening to lively Cuban music.
Take an airboat ride into the Everglades wetlands to spot wild alligators, herons and turtles in the sawgrass.

wildlife.name: American Alligator
wildlife.description: Big scaly reptile with a toothy grin that lives in the Everglades swamps near Miami.

wildlife.name: Florida Manatee
wildlife.description: Gentle round 'sea cow' that munches seagrass and floats slowly through warm coastal waters.

wildlife.name: American Flamingo
wildlife.description: Tall bright pink wading bird with long stilt legs and a curved black-tipped beak.

wildlife.name: Brown Pelican
wildlife.description: Big seabird with a stretchy throat pouch that dives head-first to scoop up fish.

localWord.meaning: Hello in Spanish, the language you'll hear most in Little Havana.

images.alt: Miami's tall sparkly downtown buildings line the bay at golden hour.
Pastel-coloured Art Deco hotels line Ocean Drive on famous South Beach.
A bright-coloured lifeguard hut on South Beach glows in the sunrise.
The fancy old Vizcaya mansion sits beside pretty green gardens by the sea.
Giant rainbow-coloured murals cover the walls in the Wynwood art district.
An airboat with a big fan zooms across the sawgrass swamps of the Everglades.
A big American alligator basks at the edge of the water in the Everglades.
A Florida manatee glides peacefully underwater.
images.credit: Keith Ramos / U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service

images.alt: A bright pink American flamingo standing tall on its long legs.
A brown pelican glides over the water with its wings outstretched.
